title: Build a Coding Bootcamp Registration app

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you will build a Coding Bootcamp Registration app where users should be able to register their accounts with their usernames and passwords.

Project: `myproject`, App: `user_app` \
Templates (already setup): `base.html` , `home.html` , `register.html` \
URLs: All mapping already set up in project level `urls.py`

#### Task: Complete the `views.py` file:
In views.py, you'll find a `register_view` function already defined.
Complete the logic inside this function so that it:
1. Uses Django's built-in UserCreationForm to handle user registration.
2. When the form is submitted (POST request):
   - Validate the form.
   - If valid, create the new user.
   - Display a success message exactly as:
     **Account created for ``. Get ready to code!**

3. After successful registration, reset the form (so it’s empty again).
4. If it’s a GET request, just show an empty registration form. \
Finally, render the register.html template, passing the form in the context.

After completing the above, click on **RUN** to start the server. \
In the homepage that loads, you should see a "Enroll Now" Link. \
Open the browser in a new tab. \
Click on it and sign up with some valid username and password of your choice to see if it is working as expected. \
You can also click on SUBMIT after clicking on RUN to check if everything was implemented as expected.
